# Transcrição: 92. Why Your Kids are Dumb

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Let's face it, everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes. So here's my two cents in under two minutes. Hey Grant Cardone here, New York Times bestselling author and dedicated father and husband. I want to tell you how I started my day today and I hope it encourages you to consider how you're starting your day. I wake up this morning, I go grab my little three-year-old Sabrina and I spend an hour with her every morning. And I think this is really important for two reasons. Number one, I want that personal time with my daughter in my most important role in my life as a father, as a role model, as somebody that is going to be an example for my little girl. I think that the most important time I can get with her is in the morning when she first wakes up, I get some food in her, she's happy, she's looking forward to the day and that's when I get to spend some time influencing her in a life and bonding with her. The second thing I do in that hour is I take responsibility for the education she's getting. Last week I read in a USA Today article that a school in Florida had such low scores on a writing test that they actually had an emergency meeting of the board, the school board, so they lowered the score to 70 so 25% more people passed the test. It was like I was like shocked, I was like amazed, I was like oh my gosh and let me tell you what I was most freaked out about. Not the fact, not the fact that so few people did well. I already know they're not getting my kids, your kids are not getting the right education in school. That's not what freaked me out. What freaked me out and hopefully what will just freaking blow the top off your head is the fact that the school board would tell kids and parents that it's alright to lower the bar. Are you kidding me man? People fail and we lower the bar? That's not America, that's not what made this country great, that's not the lesson I want my daughter Sabrina to learn. We don't lower the bar at my house baby. If you can't win, you don't win. If it's a 70, it's a 70. Now check this out. One of the teachers thought that when they went back and looked at the test, thought the writing test was unfair because one of the words in the writing test was the word camel. C-A-M-E-L. She said what kid should be, have to know what a camel is. And I'm like how old are these kids? Fourth graders. A fourth grader is basically 10 or 11 years old right? Now she said why would a 11 year old kid have to know what a camel is when these kids are from Florida and there aren't any camels in Florida? I'm like are you kidding me man? Okay, we're a world economy now. My little girl is going to have bankers that are Chinese. She's going to be communicating with people in Pakistan because she's going to be on some freaking super Twitter site or super Facebook site going forward right? My daughter knows what a camel is and she's only three.

## [03:00 - 04:11]

Why? Because I take responsibility as a parent and I'm encouraging you to do the same. Two things you need to do every day. Spend an hour with your kid every day okay? Just you and the kid. Just you and the kid. If you got to get up an hour early man, it's the most important role men and women have in their lifetime. The most important role I have in my lifetime is not to bank money, not to write a book, not to be with my wife. It's with my kids. I need to also add an hour for my wife okay? The second thing I would strongly encourage you to do is and I'm going to give you three here. The second thing is spend time educating your kids. You know they're deficient. You know the schools don't work. You know they're not getting the education. Spend some time giving your kids the education. Math, they need writing, communicating. All those things they don't teach in schools. Spend some time doing that. The second thing is you need to write your school. Write your school and say quit lowering the standards. It is ridiculous. We have to compete. My kids have to compete in a world where this past Saturday there was 20 million Chinese kids doing math classes on a Saturday. What do your kids do on a Saturday? Man, bone up. Bone up as parents. Find the time. Make the time. Commit to education. Give them what they need and tell your school system hey quit lowering the bar. Thanks a lot.
